query: Can we open a savings account for a customer who refuses to give their real identity, using a name they've chosen instead?
notes: Clause 10(a): 'No account is opened in anonymous or fictitious/benami name.'

query: What factors should we use to classify a customer as low, medium, or high risk for KYC purposes, and can we tell them their risk category?
notes: Clause 12(c) lists risk-categorisation parameters; 12(d) requires the categorisation and reasons to be kept confidential from the customer to avoid tipping off.

query: What can we accept from an individual customer as ID proof if Aadhaar offline verification isn't possible for them?
notes: Clause 16(ab): proof of possession of Aadhaar where offline verification cannot be carried out, or any OVD/equivalent e-document.

query: What are the maximum balance and transaction limits on a 'Small Account' opened without full KYC documents?
notes: Clause 23(i)-(iii): aggregate credits <= Rs.1 lakh/year, withdrawals/transfers <= Rs.10,000/month, balance <= Rs.50,000 at any time.

query: If an existing customer transfers their account to a different branch of our bank, do we need to redo their KYC?
notes: Clause 26: KYC done at one branch is valid for transfer to another branch of the same RE, provided it isn't due for periodic updation.

query: Do we need to identify and verify the beneficial owner of a company customer that is itself listed on an Indian stock exchange?
notes: Clause 34(a): not necessary to identify/verify shareholders or beneficial owners of entities listed on an Indian stock exchange (or their subsidiaries).

query: How often are we required to review a customer's risk categorisation once their account is open?
notes: Clause 37(a): periodic review of risk categorisation of accounts, at least once every six months.

query: How frequently must we ask a low-risk individual customer to update their KYC documents?
notes: Clause 38: periodic updation at least once every ten years for low-risk, eight years medium-risk, two years high-risk customers.

query: What extra approval do we need before opening an account for a politically exposed person?
notes: Clause 41(A)(c): approval to open an account for a PEP must be obtained from senior management, plus enhanced ongoing monitoring.

query: Do we need to complete individual CDD on every member of a Self Help Group before opening the group's savings account?
notes: Clause 43(a)-(b): CDD of all SHG members not required; CDD of the office bearers suffices.

query: How long must we retain a customer's identification documents after their account relationship with us ends?
notes: Clause 46(b): identification records preserved for at least five years after the business relationship has ended.

query: If we file a Suspicious Transaction Report on a customer, are we allowed to restrict or freeze their account operations because of it?
notes: Clause 49: REs shall not put any restriction on operations in the accounts merely on the basis of the STR filed.

query: Can we share a customer's account details with our own marketing team for cross-selling other products?
notes: Clause 55(b): information collected for account opening is confidential and can't be divulged for cross-selling without the customer's express permission.

query: Can we honour a cheque a customer presents to us five months after its issue date?
notes: Clause 58: payment of cheques/drafts/pay orders presented beyond three months from the instrument date shall not be made.

query: Do we have to issue a fresh Unique Customer Identification Code every time a walk-in customer does an occasional transaction with us?
notes: Clause 61(b): REs may, at their option, not issue UCIC to walk-in/occasional customers, provided frequent walk-in customers are still identified and allotted one.

query: Are we required to background-check our own staff before assigning them to KYC/AML-related roles?
notes: Clause 70(a): adequate screening mechanism, including a Know Your Employee/Staff policy, as part of recruitment/hiring.

query: Can we compound and charge interest on a term loan quarterly instead of monthly to increase our yield?
notes: Clause 4(a)(vii): interest shall be charged on all advances at monthly rests (with a carve-out for specified agricultural advances).

query: Can we offer a preferred corporate customer a loan priced below the benchmark rate for that loan's maturity?
notes: Clause 4(a)(xi): there shall be no lending below the benchmark rate for a particular maturity, for all loans linked to that benchmark.

query: How often are we required to review and publish our MCLR rates?
notes: Clause 6(b)(ix)(a): banks shall review and publish MCLR of different maturities every month on a pre-announced date.

query: Can we change our Base Rate calculation methodology mid-year if our funding costs shift?
notes: Clause 6(a)(vi): banks shall not review the Base Rate methodology for at least three years from the date of its finalisation (narrow exception for banks starting operations after Sept 2, 2013).

query: Which circular governs how we levy penal charges on a loan account, since this Direction doesn't spell it out?
notes: Clause 5: the Penal Interest paragraph was deleted; banks must follow Circular DoR.MCS.REC.28/01.01.001/2023-24 on 'Fair Lending Practice - Penal Charges in Loan Accounts.'

query: Since when have new floating-rate retail and MSE loans been required to link to an external benchmark instead of MCLR?
notes: Clause 7(a): new floating rate personal/retail loans and MSE loans from Oct 1, 2019 (Medium Enterprises from Apr 1, 2020) must be benchmarked to an approved external benchmark.

query: Can we price some home loans to a repo-rate benchmark and others to a T-bill-linked benchmark within the same loan category?
notes: Clause 7(c): a bank must adopt a uniform external benchmark within a loan category; multiple benchmarks within the same category are not allowed.

query: Can we raise an existing borrower's credit risk premium simply because market interest rates have gone up generally?
notes: Clause 8(b)(i)/8(d): credit risk premium to an existing borrower shall not be increased except due to deterioration in the customer's own credit risk profile, supported by a full risk-profile review.

query: How often can we revise the operating-cost component of spread on a loan linked to an external benchmark?
notes: Clause 8(e): under External Benchmark, other spread components (e.g. operating cost) may be altered once in three years, with a narrow carve-out for earlier reduction for customer retention.

query: What's the maximum interval allowed between interest-rate reset dates on an MCLR-linked floating rate loan?
notes: Clause 9(i)(c): the periodicity of reset shall be one year or lower, specified in the loan contract.

query: How frequently must we reset the interest rate on a loan linked to an external benchmark like the repo rate?
notes: Clause 9(ii): the interest rate under external benchmark shall be reset at least once every three months.

query: Can we charge a processing fee to a customer switching their existing BPLR-linked loan over to the Base Rate system?
notes: Clause 10: no fee shall be charged for a switch-over from BPLR to the Base Rate system.

query: If an existing MCLR-linked borrower switches their loan to an external benchmark, does that count as a foreclosure that triggers foreclosure charges?
notes: Clause 11(ii): the switch-over to External Benchmark shall not be treated as a foreclosure of the existing facility; eligible borrowers can switch without charges beyond reasonable administrative/legal costs.

query: Do the MCLR/external-benchmark pricing rules in this Direction apply to loans we give our own employees?
notes: Clause 13(d)(ii): advances to banks' own employees (including retired employees) are exempted from the Chapter III/IV provisions.

query: Does a fixed-rate loan with a five-year tenor need to be linked to MCLR or an external benchmark?
notes: Clause 13(d)(v): fixed rate loans of tenor above three years are exempted from the Chapter III/IV benchmark provisions.

query: Can we price a foreign-currency-denominated advance off our domestic MCLR?
notes: Clause 12(b): interest rates on advances in foreign currency shall be determined with reference to a market-determined EXTERNAL benchmark, not MCLR/Base Rate.

query: Is a household with an annual income of four lakh rupees eligible for a 'microfinance loan' under these directions?
notes: Clause 3.1: a microfinance loan is a collateral-free loan to a household with annual income up to Rs.3,00,000.

query: Can we secure a microfinance loan with a lien on the borrower's savings account to reduce our risk?
notes: Clause 3.3: the microfinance loan shall not be linked with a lien on the borrower's deposit account.

query: Must we offer flexible repayment schedules on microfinance loans, or can every borrower be put on a fixed monthly EMI?
notes: Clause 3.4: REs shall have a board-approved policy providing flexibility of repayment periodicity per the borrower's requirement.

query: Is there a cap on how much of a household's income can go toward repaying their loans across all lenders?
notes: Clause 5.1: board-approved policy capping loan repayment outflows at a maximum 50% of monthly household income.

query: If a household's existing loan repayments already exceed the 50% income cap, must we call in or foreclose those loans right away?
notes: Clause 5.3: such existing loans are allowed to mature rather than being called in, but no new loans may be given to the household until it's compliant.

query: Is there a specific interest rate ceiling we must stay under for microfinance loans?
notes: Clause 6.2: no numeric cap stated - rates/charges must not be usurious and are subject to RBI supervisory scrutiny.

query: Can we charge a borrower a penalty if they repay their microfinance loan earlier than scheduled?
notes: Clause 6.6: there shall be no pre-payment penalty on microfinance loans.

query: Are we required to publicly display our interest rate range for microfinance loans, or is that confidential pricing?
notes: Clause 6.7: REs must prominently display minimum, maximum and average interest rates in offices, literature, and on their website.

query: If we raise the interest rate on a microfinance loan, does the new rate apply retroactively to instalments already due?
notes: Clause 6.8: any change in interest rate/charge must be informed to the borrower in advance and take effect only prospectively.

query: Do we need to give a microfinance applicant a Key Facts Statement before they sign the loan agreement?
notes: Clause 6A.2: REs shall provide a KFS to all prospective borrowers, in a language they understand, before executing the loan contract.

query: How long is a Key Facts Statement valid before the borrower must accept the loan terms in it?
notes: Clause 6A.3: KFS validity is at least three working days for loans of tenor >= seven days, or one working day for shorter-tenor loans.

query: Can we charge a microfinance borrower a mid-loan processing fee that wasn't listed in their Key Facts Statement?
notes: Clause 6A.6: fees/charges not mentioned in the KFS cannot be charged at any stage without the borrower's explicit consent.

query: What information are we required to record on a microfinance borrower's loan card?
notes: Clause 7.1.3: loan card must include borrower ID info, pricing factsheet, all terms, repayment acknowledgements, and grievance redressal contact details.

query: If we outsource loan recovery to a third-party agency and they mishandle a case, are we still liable for it?
notes: Clause 7.3.1: outsourcing does not diminish the RE's obligations; the onus of compliance rests solely with the RE.

query: Can our recovery staff call a defaulting borrower at 7:00 a.m., or contact their employer about the debt?
notes: Clause 7.4.3(ii)/(iii): calling before 9:00 a.m./after 6:00 p.m., and harassing the borrower's relatives/friends/co-workers, are both listed as prohibited harsh recovery practices.

query: Do we need to run background checks on a recovery agency's field staff before deploying them to collect on microfinance loans?
notes: Clause 7.5.2: REs must have a due diligence process for recovery agents, including police verification of their employees' antecedents.
